title: Starbreaker 12
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:Starbreaker12ReturnsHome.jpg]] |name=''Starbreaker 12'' |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type= |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem=Equipped[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 1]] |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton=1[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]] |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed=5000 BBY[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] |retired= |battles= |affiliation=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |navy= |fleet= |owners=*[[Hok Daragon]] *[[Timar Daragon]] *[[Gav Daragon]] *[[Jori Daragon]] *[[Ssk Kahorr]] |captains= |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|This old ship isn't going to hold together much longer.|Jori Daragon|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]}} The '''''Starbreaker 12''''' was a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] owned by the [[Daragon family]] in the [[Time/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. Originally owned by husband-and-wife starship operators [[Hok Daragon|Hok]] and [[Timar Daragon]] and used as a backup to their main craft, the ''[[Shadow Runner]]'', the ship was bequeathed to their children, [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], after their [[Death/Legends|deaths]]. The Daragon siblings subsequently became [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] explorers, although they were not very successful. After one run, the ''Starbreaker 12'' was taken by local [[Aarrba's Repair Dock|repair dock]] owner [[Aarrba]] as collateral for repairs for which the Daragons were unable to pay. After narrowly escaping [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] sent to kill them after one of local [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Lord/Legends|lord]] [[Ssk Kahorr]]'s ships, the ''[[Zeta Five]]'', was destroyed on [[Goluud Corridor|one]] of the Daragons' routes, Gav and Jori Daragon were forced to steal the ''Starbreaker 12'' from Aarrba's Repair Dock and take a hyperspace jump into [[Unknown Regions/Legends|unknown territory]] to flee pursuers. The hyperspace jump eventually brought the ''Starbreaker 12'' and her crew to the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. Touching down on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] in the middle of the [[Funeral of Marka Ragnos|funeral]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]] in the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], Gav and Jori Daragon were arrested and the ''Starbreaker 12'' impounded in the [[Sith Citadel (Ziost)|Sith Citadel]] on the world of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]]. It was not long, however, until the scheming [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] launched an [[First raid on the Sith Citadel|attack]] and freed Gav and Jori Daragon, bringing them to his [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|fortress]] on [[Khar Delba]]; the ''Starbreaker 12'' was stolen and taken to Khar Delba by Sadow's [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] agents soon afterward. Sadow's rival, [[Ludo Kressh]], quickly [[Battle of Khar Delba|attacked]] Khar Delba after discovering Sadow's treachery, and Sadow allowed Jori to slip away in the ''Starbreaker 12'' during the battle, though not before planting a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] on the ship, which later led him back to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to launch an [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|attack]]. When Jori returned to her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], the ''Starbreaker 12'' was taken by Ssk Kahorr as payment for the destruction of the ''Zeta Five''. Kahorr decided to take the ship on a joyride to test its functions, but the ship was destroyed by Sadow's [[Sith invasion fleet|large Sith fleet]], which had followed the tracking device to the Republic. ==Description== The ''Starbreaker 12'' utilized a pair of [[Solar sailer/Legends|solar sails]] located on the exterior of the [[Starship/Legends|craft]] and featured a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. On the exterior of the ship, below the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]], a mass of tendril-like protrusions came out from the interior. The [[Engine/Legends|engines]] glowed yellow when ignited, and the extremities protruding from the main body of the ship were connected by wires. The [[Hull/Legends|hull]] itself was uneven and was a tan color. The cockpit had seats for the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and copilot, with the main controls laid out in front of them on a dashboard. The dashboard included the controls for the hyperdrive, which used a dial system for coordinate input. The ship also contained a dormitory area, which featured bunk beds. Below the beds was a [[Footlocker/Legends|footlocker]] containing [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and other weaponry.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]] ==History== ===Into the unknown=== {{Quote|Cinnagar Control, this is Gav Daragon on ''Starbreaker 12''. We need some urgent repairs.''"
"''What… ''again''?|Gav Daragon and Cinnagar Control|}} The ''Starbreaker 12'' was originally owned by starship operators [[Hok Daragon|Hok]] and [[Timar Daragon]], and used as a backup ship for their main ship, the ''[[Shadow Runner]]''. The Daragons had two children, [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon|Jori]], who were also aspiring starship operators. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], the [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Unification Wars]] raged in the Daragons' home [[Star system/Legends|system]], the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]], where the [[Politics/Legends|political]] leader [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] launched a crusade to unify the seven [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of the system. Teta's forces were able to unite six of the seven worlds, but their campaign stalled at the final world, [[Kirrek]], where [[Kirrek rebels|rebel forces]] [[Battle of Kirrek (Unification Wars)|encircled and besieged]] the [[Koros fleet|Tetan army]] in their citadel. With supplies running low, many starship operators, including the Daragons in the ''Shadow Runner'', began running supplies to the Tetan forces. Although Gav and Jori wished to follow them in the ''Starbreaker 12'', the ship was kept as collateral by [[Aarrba]], who ran [[Aarrba's Repair Dock]], for repairs to the ''Shadow Runner''. However, Hok and Timar Daragon did not return from this mission, as they were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by rebel gun [[Turret/Legends|turrets]]. Upon learning of their deaths, Aarrba gave the Daragon siblings the ''Starbreaker 12''. With heavy hearts, the pair vowed to explore [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the ''Starbreaker 12''. Gav and Jori Daragon then became [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] trailblazers, although they were not particularly successful. In their endeavors, the ship was usually damaged, and often had to be repaired by Aarrba. On one of their more successful runs in the ''Starbreaker 12'', the Daragons were able to map a shortcut to the [[Primus Goluud system|Goluud system]]; the route, called the [[Goluud Corridor]], was approved by the bureaucrat [[Shodon Ko]] of the [[Hyperspace Navigator's Guild|Navigators' Guild]]. However, the assigned risk factor was high, as it crossed dangerously close to the system's unstable red supergiant [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]], reducing the possibility that the route would be utilized by others. The Daragons quickly accumulated debt and gathered creditors, and at one point, they were forced to sell their dwelling in [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]] to pay for repairs to the ''Starbreaker 12''. On one run, the duo attempted to navigate the treacherous [[Starswarm Cluster/Legends|Starswarm Cluster]]. Although they succeeded in passing through the cluster, the ''Starbreaker 12'' was badly damaged, and the route they mapped was worthless. The two were left to limp home in their damaged ship and flew the ''Starbreaker 12'' to Aarrba's Repair Dock. Since the Daragons did not have enough money to pay for repairs, Aarrba kept the ship with him at his dock until the Daragons were able to pay. Meanwhile, the Daragons got themselves in a great deal of trouble. The ruthless [[Cha'a]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Lord/Legends|lord]] [[Ssk Kahorr]], furious over the loss of his ship ''[[Zeta Five]]'' on the Goluud Corridor, sent [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] after the Daragons. The [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] were able to corner the Daragons in a dead-end alley, but the Daragons were rescued by passing [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Memit Nadill]] and [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]]. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] advised them to leave Cinnagar as soon as possible, and the Daragons hatched a daring plan to steal the ''Starbreaker 12'' from Aarrba's Repair Dock and flee to [[Unknown Regions/Legends|parts unknown]]. Although the Daragons were able to easily take the ship from the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where she was being held, once in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], they were pursued by Cinnagar security forces. The ''Starbreaker 12'' was able to elude the security ships and jumped into the unknown. ===The Sith Empire=== {{Quote|Thank you for everything, Naga Sadow! With ''Starbreaker 12'', I'm sure I can make it through.|Jori Daragon during the Battle of Khar Delba|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]]}} [[File:Starbreaker12.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Starbreaker 12'']] The ''Starbreaker 12''{{'s}} flight plan took her farther than any recorded vessel coming from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] had ever gone. After a long journey, the Daragons emerged above a [[Korriban/Legends|mysterious world]]. The ''Starbreaker 12'' was deep into a section of the galaxy that was not well-mapped, although the return course was locked into the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] in case they had to return home. As they were unsure if the planet before them was inhabited, the Daragons decided to land and explore. Bringing the ''Starbreaker 12'' below the planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], the Daragons observed a great spectacle of monuments, [[Gold/Legends|gold]], and jewels. The two flew ''Starbreaker 12'' above a large canyon, and saw ships and people. The Daragons decided to land the ship in this canyon. However, the Daragons were promptly arrested by the natives. Little did they know, they had stumbled upon the fabled [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], which had been isolated for centuries. The ''Starbreaker 12'' had landed in the tomb-filled [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]]. At the moment, it was hosting the [[Funeral of Marka Ragnos|funeral]] of deceased [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]], which had degenerated into a [[Lightsaber/Legends|duel]] between rival [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]]. The ''Starbreaker 12'' was impounded in the hangar of the [[Sith Citadel (Ziost)|Sith Citadel]] on the world of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], while Gav and Jori Daragon were [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] in a small cell on the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] level. Meanwhile, a great debate raged regarding the fate of the Daragons and of the ship; Ludo Kressh wanted the Daragons executed, while Naga Sadow believed that the Daragons possessed important information and should be left alive. Eventually the decision was made to execute the Daragons. Despite this, the fate of the ''Starbreaker 12'' still hung in the balance. The ''Starbreaker 12'' was guarded by a [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]], although the slave had little ability to stop intruders. On one occasion, Naga Sadow ordered two of his [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] [[warrior]]s to take weapons from the ship; the warriors easily overpowered the guard and entered the ship, taking two blasters from a footlocker in the dormitory area. These two blasters were used in an [[First raid on the Sith Citadel|attack]] led by Sadow on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Citadel's prisoner level, covertly freeing the Daragons from their cell, and hiding them in his [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|citadel]] on the planet [[Khar Delba]]. Sadow planted the blasters at the scene to make it look like a Republic attack, sending the Sith Empire into a frenzy. Sadow took this opportunity to declare himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith. The ''Starbreaker 12'' was protected by Ludo Kressh and his personal guards in its Ziost holding bay. Soon, however, the guards [[Second raid on the Sith Citadel|came under attack]] by forces affiliated with the Dark Lord Naga Sadow. Kressh's guards were routed, and the ''Starbreaker 12'' was stolen and brought to Khar Delba. Kressh arrived at the scene after the ''Starbreaker 12'' had been stolen, and initially thought that it was a Republic attack. However, he discovered Sadow's emblem at the hangar, revealing to him that Sadow was behind the attack. On Khar Delba, Sadow brought the ''Starbreaker 12'' to Jori Daragon, whom he had kept at the citadel. An elated Daragon wanted to take the ship and return to the Republic, but Sadow warned her to be cautious. Suddenly, the citadel was [[Battle of Khar Delba|attacked]] by Ludo Kressh and his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] With Kressh and his allies [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarding]] the world from orbit, Sadow told Jori Daragon that she had to leave, without her brother. Daragon took off in the ''Starbreaker 12'', only to find herself in the midst of the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] battle. Kressh sighted the ship and ordered his [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] pilots to destroy it; however, the ''Starbreaker 12'' was able to evade them and jump into hyperspace, destined for the Koros system. Daragon remained unaware that Sadow had placed a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] on the ''Starbreaker 12''{{'s}} hull, so Sadow could eventually launch an [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|attack]] on the Republic itself. ===Return to the Republic=== {{Quote|Captain, ''Starbreaker 12'' is mine—as partial payment for my losses caused by the treachery and incompetence of this hyperspace navigator and her fugitive brother. You may check your records, Captain. I'll take possession of the ship now, if you please.|Ssk Kahorr claims possession of the ''Starbreaker 12''|}} When Jori Daragon brought the ''Starbreaker 12'' out of hyperspace over [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], the ship nearly collided with a [[Cinnagar Control]] [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]]. Despite the warnings of Cinnagar Control, Jori Daragon attempted to inform them of the Sith. However, the ''Starbreaker 12'' had been put on a wanted list after the ship's initial escape from the Koros system, and Cinnagar Control sent starfighters to capture the ship. Jori Daragon maneuvered the ''Starbreaker 12'' toward the planet's surface, causing the starfighters to fire a warning [[Laser/Legends|shot]] at the ship. Daragon continued to speed the ship toward the planet, landing it in a busy Cinnagar square. When Daragon left the ship, she was arrested by Cinnagar Control. Her tales of the Sith fell on deaf ears, and a rather large crowd gathered around the ship, including Aarrba. Also in the crowd was Ssk Kahorr, who claimed the ship was legally his. Due to a [[Merchants' Guild]] decision, the ship had been granted to Kahorr as payment for the destruction of ''Zeta Five''. Jori Daragon was detained by the Cinnagar authorities as Kahorr took possession of the ship, and she was sentenced to labor on the colony world of [[Ronika/Legends|Ronika]], a predicament from which she escaped. Now in possession of the ''Starbreaker 12'', Ssk Kahorr and his personal assistant [[Gorrm]] decided to take the ship on a joyride to test the ship's functions. Suddenly, however, a proximity alert sounded, and Gorrm found many ships dropping out of hyperspace. A [[Sith invasion fleet|large battle fleet]] appeared before them, frightening Kahorr and Gorrm. The battle fleet was commanded by Naga Sadow, who had followed the ''Starbreaker 12''{{'s}} beacon back to the Republic. Although the ''Starbreaker 12'' tried to flee, Sadow's fleet destroyed the ship. ==Commanders and crew== [[File:DaragonsAtlas.jpg|thumb|right|Gav and Jori Daragon, with the ''Starbreaker 12'']] The ''Starbreaker 12'' was originally owned by Hok and Timar Daragon, though it was used as a backup to their primary ship, the ''Shadow Runner''. After their deaths, the ship fell into the possession of their children, Gav and Jori Daragon.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]] In the Sith Empire, however, the ship was briefly commandeered by a group of Massassi warriors before possession of the ship was turned back over to Jori Daragon. After the return of Jori Daragon and the ''Starbreaker 12'' to Republic space, the ship was taken by Ssk Kahorr, who legally acquired it after the destruction of ''Zeta Five''. Ssk Kahorr, along with his personal assistant Gorrm, only got the opportunity to fly this ship once before it was destroyed. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Starbreaker 12'' first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith|''The Golden Age of the Sith'']], a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1996]]. It later appeared in ''The Golden Age of the Sith''{{'s}} follow-up arc, [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], in which the ship was destroyed. In these appearances, the ''Starbreaker 12'' was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]], and black-and-white preliminary sketches of the ship were included in the back of the first issue of ''Golden Age of the Sith''. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith
